title: Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
subhed: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

Hantavirus poses low risk to the public, WHO Europe says. What is the disease and where does it come from?
subhed: While three people have died of the disease on a cruise ship in the southern Atlantic, hantavirus presents a low risk to the public, the head of the World Health Organization Europe said Monday.

title: Sentenced in absentia, Emilie K., an IS 'returnee,' is retried in Paris
subhed: According to the National Anti-Terrorism Prosecutor's Office, Emilie K. is the first French woman repatriated after a stay in Syria to be retried, starting Monday, May 4, after having previously been sentenced in her absence.

title: Jacques Garcia, designer of French opulence
subhed: He rose to fame in the 1990s by designing the interior of Hôtel Costes in Paris. Criticized by purists, his taste for antiques and lavish style won him success with wealthy private clients and luxury hotels.

title: Tame Impala lights up Paris with Justice as surprise guests
subhed: The group led by Australian musician Kevin Parker delivered a brilliant performance at the Accor Arena on Sunday, May 3, blending psychedelic rock, pop and electronic music.

Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
subhed: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

title: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
subhed: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

title: Working while going through IVF: 'I didn't want my employers to know about it because of my career'
subhed: Women across France undergoing assisted reproductive treatment told Le Monde the challenges they face in balancing their professional lives with medical appointments.  These women shared the 'emotional and physical'  exhaustion they have tried to hide and the discrimination they have experienced.
